folio.label: 146v
folio.l: Hens ouä a myle , witú Inne a gret vilage
Bothe man and wo~man , child , hyne , and page
I trowe , his habitaciou~ be there
To ben a vysed , gret wysdom it were
Or that he dede a man , a deshonour
ña goddis armes , quod this riotour
Is it swicú ¹ile , witú hi~ for to mete
I shal hi~ seke , by weye and eke by strete
I make a vow , to goddes digne bones
Herkenetú felawes , we thre ben alle oones
Lat eche of vs , hold vp his hand to other
And eche of vs , bycome others brother
And we wol slen , this false traytour detú
He shal be slayn , he that so manye sletú
By goddes dignite , or it be nygút
To gyderes han theise thre , here trougúthes pligút
To lyue and dyen , eche of hem for other
As thougú he were , his owne I bore brother
And vp they stirte , al dronken in this rage
And fortú they gon , towardes that vilage
Of whicú the Tauänere , had spoke biforn~
And many a grisly otú , than han they sworn~
And Cristes blissed body , they to rent
Detú shal be ded , if they moun hi~ hent
¢ Whan they han gon , nat fully half a myle
Rigút as they wolde , han treden ouä a stile
An olde man , and a pore wigút , he~ mette
This olde man , ful mekely hem grette
And seide thus , now lordes god ©ow se
The proudest , of theise riotoures thre
Answerde a geyn , what karle witú sory grace
Why artow al for wrapped , saue thy face
Why lyuest thow so longe , in so gret age
This olde man gan loke , in his visage
And seide thus , for I ne can nat fynde
A man , thougú that I walked in to Ynde
Neither in Citee , ne in no vilage
That wolde chaunge his ©outhe , for myn age
And therfore mote I , han myn age stille
As longe tyme , as it is goddes wille
Ne detú allas , ne wol nat han my lyf
Thus walke I , like a restles caytif
And on the ground , whicú is my modres gate
I knokke witú my staf , bothe erly and late
And seye leue moder , lete me In
